titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the William Hurlin Papers
titlestmt.author: Andover Newton Theological School library staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday May 18, 2021 at 22:00

did.abstract: This collection documents the professional work of William Hurlin, who served as pastor for various Baptist churches in New England and was also involved in Baptist work on the state level, particularly in New Hampshire.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: William Hurlin was born July 31, 1814, in London, England. He joined a Wesleyan Methodist church in 1832, and became active in tract distribution. He preached his first sermon in 1835 in Illford, a London suburb at that time. In 1840 he became a London City Missionary in the Kingsland district, and continued service with the organization until 1849, when he moved to the United States. He had been baptized and united with a Baptist church in 1842.

In the States he was ordained pastor of a Free Baptist church in North Danville, Vermont, in 1850. He held Free Baptist pastorates in Alton, New Hampshire, Acton, Maine, and Amesbury, Massachusetts. In 1855 he affiliated with the American Baptist denomination and held the following pastorates: Acton, Maine (1856-1858), East Sumner, Maine (1858-1861), Damariscotta Mills, Maine (1861-1864), China, Maine (1864-1866), Antrim, New Hampshire (1866-1873), Plaistow, New Hampshire (1875-1877), and Goshen, New Hampshire (1877-1878).

He was active in Baptist work on the state level. He was a member of the Board of Trustees of New Hampshire Baptist Convention from 1866 until his death in 1910 and was secretary of the Convention 1878-1899. He was also treasurer and statistical secretary of the New Hampshire Baptist Ministers' Conference. Hurlin wrote a history of every New Hampshire Baptist church and prepared a brief biography of most ministers ever associated with the Convention. In addition he was a frequent contributor of articles to denominational journals.
He lived in Antrim the last 32 years of his life, and died there June 28, 1910.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred from Andover Newton Theological School Collection, 2017

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The William Hurlin Papers, 1848-1909, document the professional work of Hurlin. The collection is arranged according to the following categories: sermons, lectures, addresses, list of readings, unpublished literary manuscripts, scrapbooks of publications, and photographs. An Addendum received in 2018 includes copies of Hurlin family correspondence, an autobiographical sketch by Wiliam Hurlin, and genealogical information about the Hurlin family.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: William Hurlin Papers (RG 289), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Place names were modernized in the description, with the name originally used in the collection material or in an older version of the finding aid in parenthesis: e.g. "Beijing (Peking)" or "Benin (Dahomey)".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sermons are arranged in numerical order assigned by WH. When originally written, the numerical order was also chronological. However, the sermons in their present form are revisions of the earlier sermons. Therefore, the numerical order is no longer chronological. See the numerical list of sermons in the first folder for the original dates of the sermons.

did.unittitle: Address at Farewell Tea Meeting Kingsland
Missionary Life in Persia. and the Koordish Mountains
Address. at Bible Society at Lyndeboro
List of articles and books in alphabetical order, 2 volumes
General Catalogue of Pastors of Baptist Churches in New Hampshire from 1832 to 1892
Brief Histories of the Baptist Churches in New Hampshire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Scrapbooks contain clippings of articles by Hurlin

did.unittitle: Articles in Boston Journal and Boston Transcript

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Individuals include: Willard Ashmore, Shantou (Swatow), China; John Clifford; John E. Clough, Ongole, India; William Dean, Thailand (Siam); Mrs. William Dean, Thailand (Siam); Millard P. Johnson; Oliver Wendell Holmes; Alvah Hovey; Emory W. Hunt; Edward Judson; John F. McElwain; C.A. Nichols and son Bertie - Bassein, Burma; John R. Mott; Harrington Putnam; Walter Rauschenbusch; Augustus H. Strong (2); Willis F. Thomas [and family], Insein, Myanmar (Burma)

did.unittitle: Photograph of the Home for Children of Missionaries, Newton Centre, MA
Pictures of buildings, mostly clippings from magazines
Pictures of individuals, mostly clippings from magazines

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Material donated by John Hurlin Robinson in 2018

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies include one letter from Rev. William Hurlin to his parents, one letter from William Hurlin, Senior to his son Rev. William Hurlin, three letters to or from Grandmother Hurlin.

did.unittitle: "William Hurlin: The Weaver Boy" - autobiographical writing by Rev. William Hurlin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes long-hand version written in a pad of paper and a typed transcription prepared by John Hurlin Robinson

did.unittitle: Genealogical information - compiled by John Hurlin Robinson
